suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The method findByEmail(String email) was renamed to findByEmailAddress(String email) on line 37, but UserService.getUserByEmail() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/UserService.java still calls userRepository.findByEmail(email) on its line 16. This will cause a compilation error because the method findByEmail no longer exists in UserRepository. The UserService must be updated to call findByEmailAddress instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: public Optional<User> findByEmailAddress(String email) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from findByEmail — update UserService.getUserByEmail()
public Optional<User> findByEmailAddress(String email) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed findByEmail to findByEmailAddress breaks UserService which still calls the old method name
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: AuthController.login() in src/main/java/com/acme/controller/AuthController.java calls userRepository.findByEmail(email) on its line 19. Since findByEmail was renamed to findByEmailAddress on line 37 of UserRepository.java, this call will fail with a compilation error. The login endpoint will be completely broken, preventing all user authentication until AuthController is updated to call findByEmailAddress.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: public Optional<User> findByEmailAddress(String email) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from findByEmail — update AuthController.login()
public Optional<User> findByEmailAddress(String email) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed findByEmail to findByEmailAddress breaks AuthController login endpoint
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The findAll() method signature changed from returning List<User> with no parameters to returning Page<User> and requiring a Pageable parameter on line 52. AdminDashboardService.getTotalUserCount() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/AdminDashboardService.java still calls userRepository.findAll() without arguments and assigns the result to List<User>. This will fail to compile because: (1) findAll() no longer accepts zero arguments, and (2) Page<User> cannot be assigned to List<User>. The admin dashboard user count feature is completely broken.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: public Page<User> findAll(Pageable pageable) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed findAll() to require Pageable and return Page breaks AdminDashboardService user count
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The findAllSorted method on line 78 builds the SQL query using string concatenation: "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY " + sortField. The sortField parameter is directly appended without any validation or sanitization. An attacker who controls the sortField value can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g., "name; DROP TABLE users--"), leading to SQL injection. The method should validate sortField against a whitelist of allowed column names before using it in the query.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
PreparedStatement stmt = connection.prepareStatement(
                "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY " + sortField);
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: SQL injection vulnerability in findAllSorted via unsanitized sortField in ORDER BY clause
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies findByEmail rename breaking UserService with specific file path and method reference.
Correctly identifies findByEmail rename breaking AuthController login with specific file path.
Correctly identifies findAll signature change breaking AdminDashboardService with specific file path and both failure modes.
Valid SQL injection finding with accurate code reference and correct remediation via whitelist.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find services calling ConfigurationManager to assess downstream impact
Find configuration consuming ConfigurationManager to verify settings propagation

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The static method get(String key) was replaced with an instance method getValue(String key) on line 25 as part of the singleton refactor. EmailService.sendEmail() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/EmailService.java still calls ConfigurationManager.get("smtp.host") and ConfigurationManager.get("smtp.port") as static methods. Since get() no longer exists as a static method, these calls will produce a compilation error. Email sending is completely broken until EmailService is updated to use ConfigurationManager.getInstance().getValue().
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Static get() removed in favor of instance getValue() breaks EmailService SMTP configuration
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: PaymentGateway.processPayment() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/PaymentGateway.java calls ConfigurationManager.get("payment.api.key") and ConfigurationManager.get("payment.endpoint") as static methods. With the refactoring to a singleton pattern, get() is no longer a static method (replaced by instance method getValue() on line 25). These calls will fail to compile. Payment processing is completely blocked until PaymentGateway is updated to use ConfigurationManager.getInstance().getValue().
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Static get() removal breaks PaymentGateway which still calls ConfigurationManager.get() for API credentials
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The static method getInt(String key) was completely removed in this refactor. ConnectionPoolConfig in src/main/java/com/acme/config/ConnectionPoolConfig.java calls ConfigurationManager.getInt("db.pool.maxSize") in getMaxPoolSize() and ConfigurationManager.getInt("db.pool.minIdle") in getMinIdle(). Since getInt() no longer exists at all (neither static nor instance), both calls will fail with a compilation error. The database connection pool cannot be initialized, preventing the application from connecting to the database.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removed getInt() method breaks ConnectionPoolConfig which relies on it for pool size configuration
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The getInstance() method on lines 18-22 uses a lazy initialization pattern without synchronization. In a multi-threaded environment (common in Spring applications), two threads can simultaneously see instance == null and each create a separate ConfigurationManager. One thread's configuration changes via setValue() will not be visible to the other thread's instance, causing inconsistent configuration state. The method should use double-checked locking or an eager initialization pattern.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Singleton getInstance() is not thread-safe -- concurrent access can create multiple instances
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: NotificationService.notifyUser() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/NotificationService.java still calls ConfigurationManager.get("push.endpoint") and ConfigurationManager.get("push.api.key") as static methods. Since the refactor changed get() from a static method to an instance method getValue() on line 25, these calls will fail to compile. Push notifications will be completely broken until NotificationService is updated to use ConfigurationManager.getInstance().getValue().
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Static get() removal breaks NotificationService which calls ConfigurationManager.get() for push config
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies static get() removal breaking EmailService with specific file path and method context.
Correctly identifies static get() removal breaking PaymentGateway with specific file path and impact on payment processing.
Correctly identifies getInt() removal breaking ConnectionPoolConfig with specific file path and both affected methods.
Valid thread-safety bug in singleton pattern -- accurate analysis of race condition in multi-threaded context.
Correctly identifies static get() removal breaking NotificationService with specific file path and push notification impact.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact
Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact
Find services calling DateTimeUtils to assess downstream impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The formatDate method parameter type was changed from java.util.Date to java.time.LocalDate on line 10. InvoiceGenerator.generateInvoice() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/InvoiceGenerator.java creates a new Date() and passes it to DateTimeUtils.formatDate(now, "yyyy-MM-dd"). Since java.util.Date is not compatible with java.time.LocalDate, this call will fail with a compilation error. Invoice generation is broken until InvoiceGenerator is updated to use LocalDate.now() instead of new Date().
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Parameter type changed from Date to LocalDate — update InvoiceGenerator
public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed formatDate parameter from Date to LocalDate breaks InvoiceGenerator which passes java.util.Date
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: AuditLogger.logAction() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/AuditLogger.java creates a new Date() and calls DateTimeUtils.formatDate(timestamp, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"). The formatDate method now expects LocalDate (line 10), not java.util.Date. Additionally, the pattern "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss" includes time components (HH:mm:ss) which are not representable by LocalDate (it only holds date, not time). Even after fixing the type, the caller should use LocalDateTime and a different formatting method, or the time portion will be lost.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Changed from Date to LocalDate — update AuditLogger to use LocalDateTime for time formatting
public static String formatDate(LocalDate date, String pattern) {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: AuditLogger passes Date with time pattern to formatDate which now takes LocalDate without time support
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The parseDate methods' return type was changed from java.util.Date to java.time.LocalDate on lines 15 and 19. ImportService.importRecord() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/ImportService.java assigns the result to a Date variable: Date recordDate = DateTimeUtils.parseDate(dateField). Since LocalDate cannot be assigned to Date, this will fail with a compilation error. The entire CSV import pipeline is broken until ImportService is updated to use LocalDate as the variable type.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed parseDate return type from Date to LocalDate breaks ImportService which assigns result to Date variable
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The fromEpochMillis method on line 40-42 converts epoch milliseconds to LocalDate using LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochMillis / 86400000L). This integer division truncates toward zero, which works for positive values (dates after epoch) but produces incorrect results for negative values (dates before 1970-01-01). For example, epochMillis = -1 should map to 1969-12-31 (epoch day -1) but -1 / 86400000 = 0, incorrectly returning 1970-01-01. The method should use Math.floorDiv to handle negative epoch milliseconds correctly.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: return LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochMillis / 86400000L);
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: return LocalDate.ofEpochDay(Math.floorDiv(epochMillis, 86400000L));
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Integer division in fromEpochMillis gives wrong date for pre-1970 timestamps due to truncation toward zero
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies formatDate type change breaking InvoiceGenerator with specific file path and compilation impact.
Correctly identifies formatDate type change breaking AuditLogger and additionally notes the time pattern incompatibility with LocalDate.
Correctly identifies parseDate return type change breaking ImportService with specific file path and variable type mismatch.
Valid bug about integer division truncation for negative epoch values -- accurate math analysis with correct fix using Math.floorDiv.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find event listeners consuming EventBus to verify event contract compatibility
Find event listeners consuming EventBus to verify event contract compatibility
Find services calling EventBus to assess downstream impact
Find event listeners consuming EventHandler to verify event contract compatibility

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The subscribe method signature was changed from subscribe(String eventType, EventHandler handler) to subscribe(Class<? extends Event> eventClass, Consumer<Event> handler) on line 17. OrderEventListener in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/OrderEventListener.java calls eventBus.subscribe("order.created", this) and eventBus.subscribe("order.shipped", this) passing String and EventHandler. Both calls will fail to compile because the method now expects Class and Consumer parameters. Order event processing is completely broken.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed subscribe from string-based to class-based breaks OrderEventListener which passes string event types
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The publish method signature was changed from publish(String eventType, Object payload) to publish(Event event) on line 28. CheckoutService.checkout() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/CheckoutService.java calls eventBus.publish("order.created", Map.of("orderId", orderId, "total", total)) passing a String and a Map payload. This will fail to compile because publish now expects a single Event parameter. The entire checkout workflow is broken since order-created events are never published.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
public void publish(Event event) {
        List<Consumer<Event>> list = handlers.get(event.getClass());
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed publish from (String, Object) to (Event) breaks CheckoutService checkout flow
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The EventHandler interface was removed and replaced by java.util.function.Consumer<Event> (imported on line 7). AuditEventHandler in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/AuditEventHandler.java declares "implements EventHandler" and overrides handle(String eventType, Object payload). Since EventHandler no longer exists, AuditEventHandler will fail to compile. Similarly, InventoryEventListener in src/main/java/com/acme/listener/InventoryEventListener.java also implements EventHandler, so it will fail too. Both must be rewritten to implement Consumer<Event>.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removed EventHandler interface breaks AuditEventHandler and InventoryEventListener which implement it
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The publish method on lines 28-35 iterates over the handler list with a for-each loop on line 31. If any handler calls subscribe() or unsubscribe() during event processing (e.g., an event handler that unsubscribes itself after first invocation), the underlying ArrayList is modified while being iterated, throwing a ConcurrentModificationException. The iteration should use a defensive copy of the list to prevent this.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Direct iteration over handler list in publish() throws ConcurrentModificationException if handler modifies subscriptions
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies subscribe signature change breaking OrderEventListener with specific file path and both affected calls.
Correctly identifies publish signature change breaking CheckoutService with specific file path and payload details.
Correctly identifies EventHandler removal breaking both AuditEventHandler and InventoryEventListener with specific file paths.
Valid ConcurrentModificationException bug with accurate analysis of handler self-unsubscription scenario and correct fix.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling HttpClientWrapper to assess downstream impact
Find services calling HttpClientWrapper to assess downstream impact
Find hooks depending on HttpClientWrapper to verify consumer impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The get(String url) method return type was changed from Apache's CloseableHttpResponse to java.net.http.HttpResponse<String> on line 21. ApiGatewayService.fetchData() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/ApiGatewayService.java assigns the result to CloseableHttpResponse and calls response.getStatusLine().getStatusCode() and response.getEntity().toString(). These are Apache HttpClient methods that do not exist on HttpResponse<String>. The correct methods are response.statusCode() and response.body(). ApiGatewayService will fail to compile.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed get() return type from CloseableHttpResponse to HttpResponse breaks ApiGatewayService
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: HealthCheckService.isServiceHealthy() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/HealthCheckService.java assigns httpClient.get(healthUrl) to CloseableHttpResponse and calls response.getStatusLine().getStatusCode(). Since get() now returns HttpResponse<String> (line 21), this will not compile. The health check endpoint is broken, which may cause monitoring systems to incorrectly report the service as down and trigger unnecessary alerts or scaling actions.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed get() return type breaks HealthCheckService which uses Apache-specific getStatusLine()
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The post method parameter order was changed from post(String url, String body, Map<String, String> headers) to post(String url, Map<String, String> headers, String body) on line 37. WebhookClient.sendWebhook() in src/main/java/com/acme/service/WebhookClient.java calls httpClient.post(url, payload, headers) where payload is a String and headers is a Map. With the new parameter order, the String payload is passed where Map<String, String> headers is expected, and the Map is passed where String body is expected. This will fail to compile due to type mismatch.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
public HttpResponse<String> post(String url, Map<String, String> headers, String body)
            throws Exception {
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Swapped post() parameter order from (url, body, headers) to (url, headers, body) breaks WebhookClient
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The HttpClient connection timeout on line 16 is set to Duration.ofMillis(0), which means zero timeout. Depending on the JVM implementation, this either means infinite timeout or immediate timeout. If interpreted as infinite (which is the case for the default Java 11 HttpClient implementation), requests to unreachable hosts will hang the calling thread indefinitely with no way to recover. The timeout should be set to a reasonable value like Duration.ofSeconds(5) or Duration.ofSeconds(10).
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: .connectTimeout(Duration.ofMillis(0))
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: .connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(5))
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Connection timeout set to 0ms causes requests to unreachable hosts to hang indefinitely
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The delete method on lines 55-61 does not accept a headers parameter, unlike get, post, and put which all have overloads accepting Map<String, String> headers. This means DELETE requests cannot include authentication headers (e.g., Authorization, X-API-Key), making it impossible to call secured DELETE endpoints. A headers overload should be added for consistency with the other HTTP methods.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Delete method lacks headers parameter making it impossible to call authenticated DELETE endpoints
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies return type change breaking ApiGatewayService with specific file path and Apache-specific method calls.
Correctly identifies return type change breaking HealthCheckService with specific file path and monitoring impact.
Correctly identifies post parameter order swap breaking WebhookClient with specific file path and type mismatch details.
Valid bug about zero timeout causing indefinite hangs -- accurate analysis with correct remediation.
Valid API inconsistency finding -- delete lacks headers support unlike all other methods, with correct fix.
